text: Hey everybody! Today I'm going to be showing you my everyday blow-drying routine for my wavy hair.
I like to start off first by parting my hair down the middle.
And then I like to just brush it out a little to get all the waves out so it's nice and easy for me to work with.
I then like to section out my bangs, and then I tuck the rest of my hair back behind so it stays out of the way.
Next, I like to brush through my bangs, and then I turn on my blow-dryer.
To start off, I'm just blowing my bangs downward.
And then I like to do it kind of sideways-ish. It's down, but still not the same as the other one.
I just did that a bunch of times just to get it really nice and dry.
And then I take it over the other side of my head. This gives it a ton of volume.
If you don't do this, you should. It gives it just like that crazy volume that keeps it nice and high.
And then I go back to the other side, and I just keep doing the same thing over and over.
And I turn it one way, and I turn it the other way. And this really just helps get the strands nice and dry.
And it's also important to bring it up because that gives it, again, a ton of volume.
And I always like to finish off by blow-drying my bangs towards my face.
I feel like this really just helps them have a nice wave and sets them nicely.
Moving on to the top layer of my hair, I just start off by sectioning that out, and again, putting the rest of my hair on the other side.
And then I brush it out with the brush.
And then just hit with my brush.
And I just pull it down, and I just roll it around the barrel a lot at the ends because that gives it a really cute curl.
I repeat that step just to really start getting the hair nice and dry.
And then I pull it over the head, and this again gives it a lot of volume, and it also helps to dry the underneath, which is very important.
And then I go back and just get the side again, and I just keep repeating the same steps.
Just to really get the volume.
Just to help the hair get nice and dry and voluminous.
Again, I like to finish up by just curling my hair inward.
I feel like for my hair, this just really gives it a nice curl and sets it.
So that's how I blow-dry completely one side of my head.
And now I go onto the other side and just repeat all of those steps exactly the same.
I just do the same things over and over again, inward, outward, down, up.
A lot of fun stuff.
So now that we did the top layer of the head, it's time to flip on over and do the rest of the hair.
This is really just the easiest part.
I just flip on over and then I just brush my hair just to get it all nice and smooth and get the waves that were forming out of it.
To start off, I like to just run the brush along the outside of my hair, and then I have the blow-dryer on the inside of my hair, if that makes sense.
And I just curl it around.
I feel like this really just helps the ends seal up or something.
It makes it look really nice, and so I like to do that.
This part is pretty self-explanatory.
You just brush your hair forward over and over and over again until it's dry.
And so once I have the inside, if that makes sense, of the strands, I like to just run the blow-dryer and my brush outside.
So I just brush it forward with the blow-dryer.
And then I just curl it back up.
So that is all of my blow-dry routine for my wavy hair.
It's pretty simple. It's not that hard. There's not much of a science to it.
And so I think anyone can really do it.
I hope you enjoyed this video. If you did, give it a thumbs up, and I hope it helped you as well.
I will see you guys again very soon. Bye!
